channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: 10 die, six Injured in Ogun road crash
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tragedy strikes on the Ibadan-Lagos Expressway as 10 die and six are injured in an Ogun road crash involving a truck and a bus. FRSC blames speeding and wr
Read More: https://punchng.com/10-die-six-injured-in-ogun-road-crash/

item.title: I staged my kidnap to extort N25m from family — Delta man
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Delta State man, Michael James, confessed to staging his own kidnap to extort N25m from his family, claiming he owed money he couldn’t repay.
Read More: https://punchng.com/i-staged-my-kidnap-to-extort-n25m-from-family-delta-man/

item.title: Bandits kill nine farmers in Kaduna, kidnap many
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bandits killed nine farmers and kidnapped many others in a recent attack in Kaduna State. Learn more about the ongoing violence impacting farming communiti
Read More: https://punchng.com/bandits-kill-nine-farmers-in-kaduna-kidnap-many/

item.title: Understanding Governor Okpebholo’s Stance On The Symbols Of A Proud Civilisation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: By Dr. Patrick Akhere Ebojele When Edo State Governor Senator Monday Okpebholo declared that the returned Benin Bronzes belong to the Oba of Benin’s Palace and should never be politicised, he did far more than welcome an international delegation to Government House in Benin City. His remarks t

item.title: Okonjo-Iweala hails African teams’ impressive World Cup campaign
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
WTO DG Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala praises African teams’ resilience and performance at the 2026 World Cup, highlighting Morocco, Egypt, and Cape Verde.
Read More: https://punchng.com/okonjo-iweala-hails-african-teams-impressive-world-cup-campaign/

item.title: Maren Morris Unveils Custom Wedding Keepsake from Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce’s Big Day
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Country star Maren Morris has shared one of the most personal details from Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, giving fans a glimpse of the custom keepsake presented to guests at the exclusive ceremony. After attending the couple’s lavish wedding at Madison Square Garden in New York on Friday,

item.title: NEM Insurance, Custodian, Fidelity Bank top stock pick this week

item.title: Dangote Cement rewards shareholders with N753.8bn dividend, pays N45 per share
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Mather Godwin Dangote Cement Plc has announced a 50 per cent increase in dividend payout to shareholders, raising the dividend from N30 per share to N45 per share, which translates to a total payout of approximately N753.8 billion, reaffirming the company’s position as one of the most rewarding i

item.title: Para swimming: Adeyemi unveils ambitious revival plan, targets LA 2028 success
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria Para Swimming Federation President Adeshina Adeyemi unveils plans for grassroots growth, more competitions and LA 2028 success. The post Para swimming: Adeyemi unveils ambitious revival plan, targets LA 2028 success appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Expert Projects Mild Stock Market Recovery In H2 As Corporate Earnings Improve
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The CEO of HighCap Securities Limited, David Adonri, expects Nigeria’s equities market to gradually regain momentum in the second half of 2026, driven by stronger corporate fundamentals and better earnings prospects. Adonri stated this at the Capital Market Correspondents Association of Nigeria (CAM

item.title: Don’t sell your PVC, Oyebamiji charges Osun voters
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The flagbearer of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in the forthcoming governorship election in Osun State, Bola Oyebamiji, on Sunday, urged citizens of the state to be wary of selling their voting power and votes to desperate politicians at next month’s elections. Oyebamiji, speaking at a special

item.title: Okonjo-Iweala Showcases Dance Skills As Igbo Masquerades Perform At WTO Open Day Ceremony
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The World Trade Organisation’s annual Open Day ceremony was fun as cultures collided, but one performance stole the entire afternoon. The crowd of international delegates, ambassadors, witnessed the rhythmic and unmistakable sound of the Ogene- a metal gong paired with the deep rattle of the I

item.title: Troops Arrest Suspected Military Impostor In Katsina
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of the 17 Brigade have arrested a suspected military impostor during a fighting patrol in Malumfashi Local Government Area of Katsina State. The post Troops Arrest Suspected Military Impostor In Katsina appeared first on Trending News.

item.dc:creator: Premium Times

item.title: Firm Posts N49.1bn Net Profit In 2026
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: P Z Cussons Nigeria Plc reported a net profit of N49.1 billion for the financial year ended May 31, 2026. The consumer goods firm said the results were driven by strong brand performance, pricing discipline and efficiency gains across its operations. The Company’s full-year audited financial s

item.title: Firm Completes Share Reconstruction, Lists 3.23bn Shares
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Fortis Global Insurance Plc has completed its share capital reconstruction, listing 3.23 billion shares on the Nigerian Exchange (NGX). Share capital reconstruction is a corporate action that reduces the number of shares in issue by consolidating existing shares into fewer units. While the number of

item.title: EBRD targets $1.5bn investments in Nigeria over 3 years
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) has set a target of investing at least $1.5 billion in Nigeria over the next three years as it expands its operations in Sub-Saharan Africa. The projection was disclosed by the EBRD’s Managing Director for Sub-Saharan Africa, Ms Heike Harm

item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Distribute LPG Cylinders to Abuja Communities IHS Nigeria, in partnership with the Federal Capital Territory Health Services and Environment Secretariat, has concluded the Project Breathe Clean Air Abuja. Regarded as a landmark initiative, it was designed to help the underserved communities across t

item.title: VIDEO: Things are getting better for very poor people in Nigeria — Tinubu’s aide
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s Special Adviser on Economic Affairs, Tope Fasua, has stated that the living conditions are improving for Nigeria’s poorest population, as the Federal Government allocates more resources to states and local governments. Fasua made the remarks during an interview with Seu

item.title: 2026 World Cup will be my last tournament, says Cristiano Ronaldo
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cristiano Ronaldo confirms the 2026 World Cup will be his final tournament, expressing his focus on enjoying every moment and leaving with no regrets.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Morocco have strengthened their credentials as serious contenders for the 2026 FIFA World Cup after brushing aside co-hosts Canada 3-0 to book a place in the quarter-finals, continuing a remarkable run that has established them as one of the tournament’s standout teams. The Atlas Lions may not have

item.title: Osun guber: Don’t sell your votes, Oyebamiji begs voters
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) candidate in the August 15 Osun State governorship election, Bola Oyebamiji, has appealed to residents not to sell their votes during the poll. Oyebamiji urged voters to make good use of their Permanent Voter Cards by electing a leader they believe can govern the

item.title: Ooni, Owa Obokun, others eulogise Ibidapo-Obe at technical university launch
item.dc:creator: Tribune

item.title: Abu Dhabi Wealth Fund to Take TAQA Private
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Abu Dhabi has spent years building one of the Gulf’s fastest-growing stock markets through a wave of blockbuster IPOs, only to now take one of its biggest companies private. Bloomberg's Laura Gardner Cuesta reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Spain, Portugal Set For Blockbuster Iberian Showdown In Last 16
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage serves up one of its most eagerly anticipated fixtures on Monday as fierce Iberian rivals Spain and Portugal renew hostilities in Arlington with a place in the quarter-finals at stake. Eight years after producing a memorable 3-3 draw at the 2018 World Cup in Ru

item.description: By Yinka Kolawole Nigeria is positioning its vast critical mineral deposits as the foundation for a new wave of industrial growth, following the unveiling of a strategic roadmap designed to convert the country’s mineral wealth into investments in clean energy manufacturing and domestic value additio

item.title: How I was branded cultist, extorted N700k by cops – Lagos resident
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A Lagos resident, identified as Osasi, has alleged that he was branded a cultist and extorted of about ₦700,000 by men who identified themselves as police officers in Ikorodu, describing the experience as one of the most traumatic of his life. The post How I was branded cultist, extorted N700k by co

item.title: Presidency: Importers have benefited enough from Nigeria’s economy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Presidency has defended the Federal Government’s economic reforms, saying businesses that rely heavily on imports have enjoyed significant gains over the years and that the country must now shift its focus toward expanding exports. The post Presidency: Importers have benefited enough from Nigeri

item.title: GoNigeria Urges Millions Of First-Time Voters To Register Before July 24 Deadline
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: GoNigeria, a non-partisan civic initiative, has launched a nationwide campaign urging millions of young Nigerians who recently turned 18 to register to vote before the voter registration deadline of Friday, 24 July 2026. The post GoNigeria Urges Millions Of First-Time Voters To Register Before July

item.dc:creator: Leadership

The appointment comes as the NSC continues to implement reforms aimed at strengt

item.title: The Philosophy Of Logic In War
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: This article is an abstract rendition of the dynamics of war. When two warring factions engage in battle, their ultimate aim is victory. How that victory is achieved is a combination of factors. Tactics, strategy, and execution form the tripod that makes or mars an operation. Have we wondered why sm

item.title: Manufacturers embrace local sourcing, value addition to cut import dependence
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Yinka Kolawole Nigeria’s manufacturers are increasingly embracing local sourcing and value addition as a survival strategy to reduce dependence on imported inputs amid rising production costs that continue to undermine their competitiveness within the domestic and regional markets. item.description: The Ansar-u-Deen Society in Osun State has reaffirmed its non-partisan position while receiving the governorship candidate of the All Progressives Congress, APC, Bola Oyebamiji, ahead of the August 15 governorship election in the state. The assurance was given during a meeting between the

item.description: Across Nigeria’s 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory, these are the top five Nigerian news stories you shouldn’t miss. item.title: 2027: Politicians spending quality time discussing mundane issues —Prof Kila
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: DIRECTOR, Commonwealth Institute of Advanced and Professional Studies (CIAPS), Professor Anthony Kila, has warned politicians against relegating fundamental issues concerning Nigeria’s future for mundane issues less than seven months to the general election. Professor Kila, who spoke to the Nigerian

item.title: 'Not good enough!' Tuchel fumes at refs after 'heroic' England win
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Thomas Tuchel hit out at officials and praised England's "heroic" performance in their thrilling 3-2 win over Mexico in the World Cup last 16.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Minister of Works, David Umahi, has said President Bola Tinubu is using road rehabilitation and construction projects under his administration to tackle insecurity in Nigeria. item.title: OPEC+ Agrees To Increase Oil Output By 188,000 Barrels Per Day In August
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Seven OPEC+ countries have agreed to increase oil production by 188,000 barrels per day in August 2026 as part of a gradual easing of the additional voluntary output cuts introduced in 2023. item.description: Nigeria’s youngest chartered accountant, Osasere Okundaye, has recounted how she began her journey with the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria, ICAN, at the age of 13, after finishing secondary school early. Okundaye spoke on Saturday during the Power Must Change Hands monthly programme a

item.title: Ocado Says Co-Founder Tim Steiner to Step Down as CEO in 2028
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Ocado Group Plc Chief Executive Office Tim Steiner will step down at the start of its 2028 financial year, as the British online grocery delivery company grapples with a series of setbacks and a stock slump.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had warned of a likely attack ahead of meeting with US President Donald Trump.

item.title: 2027: Jonathan breaks silence on alleged N500 billion offer to contest against Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan says he was never offered N500 billion to contest against the National Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi, in the 2027 general election to divide votes in the South-South region. item.dc:creator: Daily Post

item.title: Insecurity: Over 30 villages abandoned in Sokoto – ADC candidate raises concern
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The governorship candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Sokoto State, Manir Muhammad Dan’iya, has expressed concern over reports that more than 30 villages in Danchadi District of Bodinga Local Government Area have been deserted due to persistent bandit attacks. Dan’iya, p

item.title: Neymar says Brazil career over after Norway defeat
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: An emotional Neymar says his international career for Brazil is "over" following his side's exit at the 2026 World Cup at the hands of Norway.

item.title: Khamenei funeral procession begins in Tehran, draws massive crowds of mourners
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The funeral procession for Iran's late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei set out Monday morning through the streets of Tehran, according to state television, on the third day of a state funeral intended by the Iranian government to be a show of strength and unity.

item.title: Ronaldo: World Cup 2026 Will Be My Last Tournament
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Portugal star Cristiano Ronaldo has officially confirmed that the ongoing 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the final World Cup appearance of his illustrious career. Speaking ahead of Portugal’s Round of 16 clash against Spain, the 41-year-old stated he is at peace with his legacy and wants to enjoy

item.title: Europe Has Replaced Most US Cuts Within NATO, Top Commander Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: European NATO allies have mostly replaced the assets that the US has cut from its rescue plans in case of a war in Europe, Deputy Supreme Allied Commander Europe Sir John Stringer told Bloomberg TV in an interview. “European allies have definitely stepped up in terms of backfilling the adjustment in

item.description: Nigerian troops have recovered 50 rustled cattle after foiling a cattle rustling operation in Chikun Local Government Area of Kaduna State. item.title: FCMB shareholders approve N23.1bn dividend
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Shareholders of FCMB Group Plc have approved a total dividend payout of N23.08 billion for the 2025 financial year at the company’s 13th Annual General Meeting (AGM) held in Lagos last week. Shareholders, attending both in person and online, approved all Board resolutions, including the re-election

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Police in Cross River State have confirmed that a man named Charles Ekuma Eze, 36, in Ikom in central part of the state was stabbed with a dagger following an argument with one Micheal Ndifon, 42. The incident happened at Etayip community in Ikom Town, Ikom LGA over the weekend. However, th

item.title: NELFUND investigates 34 institutions over unpaid student loan refunds
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigerian Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) has launched investigations into about 34 tertiary institutions over allegations that they failed to refund students whose tuition fees were paid twice under the Federal Government’s student loan scheme. item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: PZ Cussons Nigeria Plc reported a pre-tax profit of N77.32 billion for the year-to-date period ended May 31, 2026, representing a 364.08% increase from N16.66 billion recorded in the same period of 2025. item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Efe Onodjae The Executive Secretary and Chief Executive Officer of the Nigerian Shippers’ Council (NSC), Dr. Akutah Pius, says the Council protects over N90.60 billion and $1.348 million in economic value for Nigerian shippers through regulatory interventions and dispute resolution. item.title: Sudan’s maternity wards reopen, bringing hope amid post-war struggles
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Sudan’s maternity wards reopen, bringing hope and safe deliveries to mothers navigating post-war economic hardships.

item.title: AI Cracks Risk Ruining Europe’s Goldilocks Moment
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Europe is enjoying a breakout moment as the major beneficiary of a global rotation out of technology stocks. That said, its immunity against cracks in the artificial-intelligence trade is unlikely to last.

item.title: Johannesburg Sees Path to Ratings Upgrade From Moody’s Outlook
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The City of Johannesburg, which is on the verge of financial collapse, said Moody’s Ratings’ positive outlook on the municipality’s credit assessment provides a path to an upgrade that the authorities will seek to achieve through better governance.

item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [DW] The army claimed it had regained control after the coordinated assaults on several towns and cities by Tuareg rebels and Islamists.

item.title: Nigeria, 8 other countries account for 83% of global gas flaring —Report
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigeria has been named among eight other countries that accounted for 83 percent or more than four-fifths of global gas flaring in 2025, while producing only 46 per cent of the world’s oil. Published by the World Bank’s Global Flaring and Methane Reduction Partnership, in collaboration with the Payn

item.title: Anti-immigration violence threatens Africa’s integration drive says AfCFTA head
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Wamkele Mene, secretary-general of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) secretariat, has condemned attacks on foreign nationals in South read more Anti-immigration violence threatens Africa’s integration drive says AfCFTA head

Senate clarifies State Police Bill safeguards, allowing federal intervention in electoral intimidation and human rights abuses, dismissing concerns of gube

item.description: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. item.title: 5 foods diabetic patients should avoid
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Diabetes is a condition that affects how the body uses sugar. For people living with diabetes, what they eat plays an important role in controlling blood sugar levels and preventing complications. The post 5 foods diabetic patients should avoid appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: W’Cup qualifiers: D’Tigers end perfect window with Rwanda rout
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
D’Tigers secure passage to the second round of the FIBA Basketball World Cup African Qualifiers after a perfect window, including a rout of Rwanda.
Read More: https://punchng.com/wcup-qualifiers-dtigers-end-perfect-window-with-rwanda-rout/

item.title: Oil Slips, Markets Mixed As Tech Recovery Stutters
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Tankers continued to pass through the Strait of Hormuz with optimism over US-Iran peace talks. The post Oil Slips, Markets Mixed As Tech Recovery Stutters appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: How Africa Can Finally Turn its Digital Footprint into Economic Power
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: | By: Moses Kipkogei Every day, the world generates 2.5 quintillion bytes of data. Africa, home to 1.4 billion people and one of the fastest growing mobile populations on earth, contributes a significant and rising share of that torrent. By 2030, the continent’s data economy is projected

item.title: Africa’s biggest solar mini-grid firm plans Nigeria expansion
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: WeLight, which operates more solar mini-grids in Africa than any other company, is planning a $650 million expansion to grow read more Africa’s biggest solar mini-grid firm plans Nigeria expansion

item.title: Nigeria risks missing AI economic gains without strong regulation, CarbonAI CEO warns
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Founder and Chief Executive Officer of CarbonAI, Debola Ibiyode, has warned that Nigeria could undermine the economic potential of artificial intelligence (AI) if it fails to establish strong regulatory guardrails. The post Nigeria risks missing AI economic gains without strong regulation, Carbo

item.title: Presidency to deploy over 100 Northern influencers to amplify Tinubu’s achievements
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The workshop was organized to equip influencers with the skills to counter disinformation and misinformation.

item.title: At least 10 killed in second Russian strikes on Kyiv in a week
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The capital's top military administrator said 46 people have been injured in the attack, including five children.

item.title: How lawfare is strangling politics in Africa
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: From Zimbabwe to Nigeria, politicians are using the courts to shut down dissent, trading tanks and coups for lawsuits and constitutional loopholes.

item.title: Monday Spontaneous Post
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: #GoodMorningMonday #NewWeekNewEnergy #FreshStart #PositiveVibesOnly #ItIsWhatItIsGood Morning...This new week will not break you. item.title: How DSS Spyware acquisition enables illegal wiretaps and detentions
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: FIRST, the phones pinged. Then the knocks came. For suspects apprehended by operatives of the Department of State Services (DSS) since 2021 and taken to the military detention facility at Wawa, a border town in Niger State, the pattern was identical: strange calls, dropped signals, then arrests with

item.title: England's Henderson injures wrist in World Cup celebrations
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England midfielder Jordan Henderson suffered a "really bad" while celebrating his side's World Cup win over Mexico, head coach Thomas Tuchel says.
